prevalence.trend: stable low since the post-2016 period

riskFactors: travel to areas with active Zika transmission
vulnerablePopulations: women of reproductive age traveling to endemic areas
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: PCR early in infection; serology in later phase with cross-reactivity considerations; case definitions per ECDC guidance

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; targeted testing in pregnant travelers and symptomatic returning travelers
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Supportive care; prevention via travel guidance and pregnancy risk counseling

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Reference laboratory networks with confirmatory testing; travel medicine clinics are common
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care; pregnancy follow-up protocols for exposed pregnancies
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Maintaining clinician awareness in low-incidence settings and managing pregnancy exposure counseling
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong travel medicine infrastructure and robust reference lab capacity for arboviruses
